title: Corrupt xlsx after updating
author: hbostic

comments.author: ezintz
rickmed
comments.body:
I'm getting corrupt files also v0.2.3

EDITED: Nevermind. In my case it was because I was a adding a sheet which already existed -it's a bug noted in another issue.

comments.author: issame
comments.body: I am also having this issue using the last version of exceljs

comments.author: IonicaBizau
guyonroche
comments.body: Could someone attach a sample input.xlsx file I could use to test this? I've tried the above code on a simple sheet and it works ok

comments.author: IonicaBizau
comments.body: @guyonroche Just any `xlsx` file. I remember I was creating it in LibreOffice from scratch and it was just not working.

comments.author: SergioDonati
comments.body:
I have same issue. After read the latest comments i make a test.
Make a simple script that only read and write on different file.

First test:
1. Create new file with LibreOffice
2. run the script
3. file corrupted

Second test:
1. Create new file with Google Docs and download as xlsx
2. run the script
3. works!! 

So something is different in LibreOffice/OpenOffice files.

comments.author: stephengardner
comments.body: Nothing here worked as an intended solution for me.  Any updates?

comments.author: vodkatad
comments.body: The same phenomenon described by SergioDonati happens to me (version 0.7.1).

comments.author: hytromo
comments.body:
I can confirm that this is happening for any libreoffice-generated xlsx I read and then write (even if no changes are made)

Libreoffice info:
```
Version: 5.4.2.2
Build ID: 1:5.4.2-0ubuntu0.17.10.1
CPU threads: 8; OS: Linux 4.13; UI render: default; VCL: kde4; 
Locale: en-US (en_US.UTF-8); Calc: group
```

comments.author: dstoyanoff
mercmobily
comments.body: I can sadly confirm the problem. Wasted 6 hours on this...

comments.author: MusicDev33
comments.body: Experiencing this problem as well. If I open the file and just write it without changing it, nothing goes wrong, but if I change one cell, I get this error.

comments.author: mercmobily
comments.body:
I solved the problem by using:

https://www.npmjs.com/package/xlsx-populate

All sorts of small formatting problems simply disappeared.
I cannot recommend it enough.

comments.author: MusicDev33
comments.body: @mercmobily Are you using this as a replacement for ExcelJS or are you using the data validation part of xlsx-populate?

comments.author: mercmobily
comments.body: I am using it to take an Excel sheet, use it as a template, and change some of the cells programmatically.

comments.author: mercmobily
comments.body: No, no, I wrote "I cannot recommend it enough". Meaning, I DO recommend it

comments.author: ConstantinTuca
comments.body:
> I solved the problem by using:
> 
> https://www.npmjs.com/package/xlsx-populate
> 
> All sorts of small formatting problems simply disappeared. I cannot recommend it enough.

I also had the same problem and **xlsx-populate** solved it. Thank you
